customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety. John is a self-initiated, cooperative inquirer with an 800+ credit score and clear bill pain ($500 summer peaks with Pepco). No skepticism signals, no competing objections. Primary risk is an unaddressed Tesla product expectation — he opened the call asking about 'Tesla solar panels' and the expectation gap was not resolved at the setter stage (codex-compliant; closer must address it).
unresolved_concerns: Tesla product expectation: John asked about 'Tesla solar panels' — he may expect Tesla-branded panels at the proposal. Closer should address the Ion/Tesla relationship early per codex reframe guidance (context.tesla_relationship_clarification).
Bill email receipt unconfirmed: John said he sent the bill at 06:15 but Parker did not confirm receipt before ending the call. Closer should verify bill data is in the system before the appointment.
sensitive_topics_raised: None. No vulnerability disclosures, no scam references, no financial hardship signals.
exact_promises_rep_made: In-person specialist visit tomorrow (day after call) at 3pm.
Appointment blocked for one hour so John does not feel rushed.
Parker will text John his personal number after the call with appointment details.
If the design doesn't make sense, 'no harm, no foul' — no pressure to proceed.
closer_should_know: John opened the call asking about 'Tesla solar panels' — address the Ion/Tesla relationship (Tesla as lead source, Ion panels, Powerwall battery option) early in the proposal before presenting the design, or the product mismatch will surface mid-close.
